title: The Fool's Leap
subtitle: Introduction & Foundations
description: Begin your tarot journey with an open heart and curious mind. Learn the basics of the tarot deck, card anatomy, and how to establish your personal practice.
objectives: Understand the structure of a tarot deck
Learn card anatomy and symbolism
Establish your personal tarot practice
Complete your first intuitive card pull
discussion: What drew you to tarot? What do you hope to learn?
How do you feel about intuition as a tool for insight?
What is your relationship with symbolism and imagery?

title: Archetypes of the Soul
subtitle: The Major Arcana
description: Journey through the 22 Major Arcana cards and discover the universal archetypes that mirror the stages of our lives. From the innocent Fool to the triumphant World, learn to read the grand narrative of your soul's journey.
objectives: Learn all 22 Major Arcana cards and their meanings
Understand the Fool's Journey as a life narrative
Connect archetypes to personal life experiences
Practice reading Major Arcana in context
discussion: Which Major Arcana card resonates most with your current life chapter?
How do you see the Fool's Journey reflected in your own life?
Which archetype feels most challenging for you to embody?

title: The Four Sacred Suits
subtitle: The Minor Arcana
description: Dive into the 56 Minor Arcana cards across four elemental suits. Wands ignite your passion, Cups flow with emotion, Swords cut through illusion, and Pentacles ground you in earthly reality.
objectives: Master all four suits and their elemental correspondences
Learn the numerological significance of each card number
Understand how suits relate to daily life situations
Read complete Minor Arcana spreads confidently
discussion: Which suit feels most dominant in your life right now?
How do the four elements show up in your daily experiences?
Which suit do you find most challenging to interpret?

title: The Royal Court
subtitle: Court Cards Deep Dive
description: Meet the Pages, Knights, Queens, and Kings of the tarot court. Learn to identify these personality archetypes in yourself and others, and discover how they show up as people, energies, or aspects of yourself in readings.
objectives: Distinguish between the four court card ranks
Identify court card personalities in real people
Recognize when court cards represent yourself vs. others
Use court cards for character and personality readings
discussion: Which court card most represents you right now? Why?
How do different court card energies show up in your relationships?
Which court card archetype do you aspire to embody?

title: Weaving the Web
subtitle: Tarot Spreads & Layouts
description: Learn to construct meaningful tarot spreads from simple three-card pulls to the classic Celtic Cross. Discover how spread design shapes the story the cards tell, and create your own personal spreads.
objectives: Master classic tarot spreads including Celtic Cross
Understand positional meanings in spreads
Design your own custom tarot spreads
Synthesize multiple cards into cohesive readings
discussion: What spread format feels most natural to your reading style?
How does the context of positions change card meanings for you?
What kind of questions do you most want to ask the cards?

title: The Fearless Reader
subtitle: Intuitive Reading Mastery
description: Break free from memorization and step into fearless, intuitive reading. Learn to trust your inner knowing, synthesize card combinations, work with reversals, and develop your unique reading voice and style.
objectives: Read cards intuitively without constant reference to books
Interpret card combinations and contextual meanings
Work confidently with reversed cards
Develop your unique reading voice and style
discussion: When do you trust your intuition vs. defer to traditional meanings?
How do you handle reversals in your personal practice?
What makes a reading feel meaningful and authentic to you?

title: Reading for Others
subtitle: Ethics, Practice & Sharing Your Gift
description: Step into your role as a reader for others. Learn the ethics of tarot reading, how to create sacred space, ask powerful questions, deliver messages with compassion, and handle challenging readings with grace and integrity.
objectives: Understand the ethics and responsibilities of reading for others
Create a sacred, safe space for readings
Deliver difficult messages with compassion and care
Establish your reading practice and boundaries
discussion: What ethical guidelines feel most important in your reading practice?
How do you handle it when someone gets a card they fear?
What boundaries do you need to set to read for others sustainably?
